The frontier says hydrate_source [S25] relation=- direction=- candidates=1 priority=121 via session.hydrate(...). S25 is probably a short id? In earlier history they used S3 etc. But short ids look like F1, S3, D2, R1. S25 is not short id format. However they said short ids are stable within this turn and look like F1, S3, D2, or R1. So we need to refer to nodes by short ids. We need to map S25 to a short id? Maybe they used S3 earlier for something else. S25 is not a short id but maybe they use S25 as a node identifier in the frontier but not a short id. The instruction says "Use exact short ids as provided in the prompt when referring to graph nodes." The prompt includes the graph frontier with "hydrate_source [S25]". That is a node identifier, but is it a short id? It starts with S, but not a single digit. Might be considered a short id? The rule says short ids look like F1, S3, D2, or R1. So S25 does not match. Perhaps they want to use the symbol identifier? But they said "exact short ids as provided in the prompt". The only short ids visible are F1, S3, D2, R1 earlier. Also maybe other nodes have short ids like S25 not listed. Could be that the frontier uses S25 as a placeholder for a node but not a short id; maybe they want to use the full selector.

Better to use graph.resolve or graph.find to get the node? But we can just call session.hydrate with target selector equal to "symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION". That is the full selector.

Thus tool_call payload: {"target":"symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION"} maybe also include other optional fields? The contract says payload is just arbitrary? It says payload: {}. But we can include target.

We need to include acquired_fact_ids: list of fact ids we acquire? Not yet acquired any fact. Could be empty list [].

Readiness_state: we are not ready to edit yet, maybe "evidence_acquired"? Actually we haven't acquired evidence yet. So readiness_state maybe "evidence_visible"? Or "not_ready"? The field can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we are about to hydrate source to get evidence, maybe after that we will have evidence. So currently readiness is "evidence_visible"? But we haven't yet seen evidence. Probably "not_ready". Let's use "not_ready".

assistant_message: brief status, e.g., "Hydrating source for TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION". Provide short.

Thus JSON:

  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Hydrating source for TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION",
  "tool_call":{

      "target":"symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION"

  },

Make sure no extra fields. No markdown. Ensure JSON is valid.

Check that tool_name must exactly match registered name: "session.hydrate". Yes.

Return only JSON.
